heading: Allotment to States
label: 3024 Allotment to States

Amendments2020—Subsec. (a)(3)(C). , made technical correction in original Act to quotation marks around “State”.Pub. L. 116–131, § 701(6)Subsec. (a)(3)(D). , struck out subpar. (D) which related to amounts alloted to States based on appropriations from fiscal years 2020 to 2029 relative to those from fiscal year 2019.Pub. L. 116–131, § 204(b), amended subpar. (D) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (D) read as follows:Pub. L. 116–131, § 204(a)“(i) For each of fiscal years 2017 through 2019, no State shall be allotted an amount that is less than 99 percent of the amount allotted to such State for the previous fiscal year.“(ii) For fiscal year 2020 and each subsequent fiscal year, no State shall be allotted an amount that is less than 100 percent of the amount allotted to such State for fiscal year 2019.”Subsec. (a)(5). , substituted “as required by paragraph (3)” for “of the prior year”.Pub. L. 116–131, § 204(c)Subsec. (d)(1)(B). , struck out “(excluding any amount attributable to funds appropriated under )” after “such amount”.Pub. L. 116–131, § 701(7)section 3023(a)(3) of this title2016—Subsec. (a)(3)(D). , added subpar. (D) and struck out former subpar. (D) which related to allotments and increases for fiscal years 2007 to 2010 based on fiscal year 2006.Pub. L. 114–144, § 4(b)(1)Subsec. (b). , struck out “subpart I of” before “part E”.Pub. L. 114–144, § 4(b)(2)2006—Subsec. (a)(3)(D). amended subpar. (D) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (D) read as follows: “No State shall be allotted less than the total amount allotted to the State for fiscal year 2000 and no State shall receive a percentage increase above the fiscal year 2000 allotment that is less than 20 percent of the percentage increase above the fiscal year 2000 allotments for all of the States.”Pub. L. 109–3652000—Subsec. (a). , added subsec. (a) and struck out former subsec. (a) which read as follows:Pub. L. 106–501, § 303(a)“(a)(1) Subject to paragraphs (2) and (3), from the sums appropriated under for each fiscal year, each State shall be allotted an amount which bears the same ratio to such sums as the population of older individuals in such State bears to the population of older individuals in all States, except that (A) no State shall be allotted less than one-half of 1 percent of the sum appropriated for the fiscal year for which the determination is made; (B) Guam, the United States Virgin Islands, and the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, shall each be allotted not less than one-fourth of 1 percent of the sum appropriated for the fiscal year for which the determination is made; and (C) American Samoa and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands shall each be allotted not less than one-sixteenth of 1 percent of the sum appropriated for the fiscal year for which the determination is made. For the purposes of paragraph (3) and the exception contained in subparagraph (A) only, the term ‘State’ does not include Guam, American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.section 3023 of this title“(2) No State shall be allotted less than the total amount allotted to the State under paragraph (1) of this subsection and for fiscal year 1987.section 3028 of this title“(3) No State shall be allotted, from the amount appropriated under , less than $50,000 for any fiscal year.section 3023(g) of this title“(4) The number of individuals aged 60 or older in any State and in all States shall be determined by the Assistant Secretary on the basis of the most recent data available from the Bureau of the Census, and other reliable demographic data satisfactory to the Assistant Secretary.”Subsec. children.text: From the sums appropriated under subsections (a) through (d) of for each fiscal year, each State shall be allotted an amount which bears the same ratio to such sums as the population of older individuals in such State bears to the population of older individuals in all States.section 3023 of this title

In determining the amounts allotted to States from the sums appropriated under for a fiscal year, the Assistant Secretary shall first determine the amount allotted to each State under paragraph (1) and then proportionately adjust such amounts, if necessary, to meet the requirements of paragraph (3).section 3023 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: No State shall be allotted less than ½ of 1 percent of the sum appropriated for the fiscal year for which the determination is made.

children.label: B
children.text: Guam and the United States Virgin Islands shall each be allotted not less than ¼ of 1 percent of the sum appropriated for the fiscal year for which the determination is made.

children.label: C
children.text: American Samoa and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands shall each be allotted not less than ⁄ of 1 percent of the sum appropriated for the fiscal year for which the determination is made. For the purposes of the exception contained in subparagraph (A) only, the term “State” does not include Guam, American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.116

children.label: I
children.text: the term “allot” means allot under this subsection from a sum appropriated under section 3023(a) or 3023(b)(1) of this title, as the case may be; and

children.label: II
children.text: the term “covered fiscal year” means any of fiscal years 2020 through 2029.

children.label: I
children.text: for fiscal year 2020, 99.75 percent of the State’s allotment from the corresponding sum appropriated for fiscal year 2019;

children.label: II
children.text: for fiscal year 2021, 99.50 percent of that allotment;

children.label: III
children.text: for fiscal year 2022, 99.25 percent of that allotment;

children.label: IV
children.text: for fiscal year 2023, 99.00 percent of that allotment;

children.label: V
children.text: for fiscal year 2024, 98.75 percent of that allotment;

children.label: VI
children.text: for fiscal year 2025, 98.50 percent of that allotment;

children.label: VII
children.text: for fiscal year 2026, 98.25 percent of that allotment;

children.label: VIII
children.text: for fiscal year 2027, 98.00 percent of that allotment;

children.label: IX
children.text: for fiscal year 2028, 97.75 percent of that allotment; and

children.label: X
children.text: for fiscal year 2029, 97.50 percent of that allotment.

children.label: I
children.text: From the portion equal to the corresponding sum appropriated for fiscal year 2019, amounts shall be allotted in accordance with paragraphs (1) and (2), and subparagraphs (A) through (C) as applicable, but no State shall be allotted an amount that is less than the percentage specified in clause (ii), for that particular year, of the State’s allotment from the corresponding sum appropriated for fiscal year 2019.

children.label: II
children.text: From the remainder, amounts shall be allotted in accordance with paragraph (1), subparagraphs (A) through (C) as applicable, and paragraph (2) to the extent needed to meet the requirements of those subparagraphs.

The number of individuals aged 60 or older in any State and in all States shall be determined by the Assistant Secretary on the basis of the most recent data available from the Bureau of the Census, and other reliable demographic data satisfactory to the Assistant Secretary.

State allotments for a fiscal year under this section shall be proportionally reduced to the extent that appropriations may be insufficient to provide the full allotments as required by paragraph (3).

children.heading: Unused funds
children.label: b Unused funds
children.text: Whenever the Assistant Secretary determines that any amount allotted to a State under part B or C, or part E, for a fiscal year under this section will not be used by such State for carrying out the purpose for which the allotment was made, the Assistant Secretary shall make such allotment available for carrying out such purpose to one or more other States to the extent the Assistant Secretary determines that such other States will be able to use such additional amount for carrying out such purpose. Any amount made available to a State from an appropriation for a fiscal year in accordance with the preceding sentence shall, for purposes of this subchapter, be regarded as part of such State’s allotment (as determined under subsection (a)) for such year, but shall remain available until the end of the succeeding fiscal year.

children.heading: Withholding of funds; disbursement
children.label: c Withholding of funds; disbursement
children.text: If the Assistant Secretary finds that any State has failed to qualify under the State plan requirements of or the Assistant Secretary does not approve the funding formula required under , the Assistant Secretary shall withhold the allotment of funds to such State referred to in subsection (a). The Assistant Secretary shall disburse the funds so withheld directly to any public or private nonprofit institution or organization, agency, or political subdivision of such State submitting an approved plan under , which includes an agreement that any such payment shall be matched in the proportion determined under subsection (d)(1)(D) for such State, by funds or in-kind resources from non-Federal sources.section 3027 of this titlesection 3025(a)(2)(C) of this titlesection 3027 of this title

children.heading: Costs of administration, ombudsman program, demonstration projects, supportive services, senior centers and nutrition services; payment and determination of non-Federal share
children.label: d Costs of administration, ombudsman program, demonstration projects, supportive services, senior centers and nutrition services; payment and determination of non-Federal share
A
children.text: such amount as the State agency determines, but not more than 10 percent thereof, shall be available for paying such percentage as the agency determines, but not more than 75 percent, of the cost of administration of area plans;

children.label: B
children.text: such amount as the State agency determines to be adequate for conducting an effective ombudsman program under shall be available for conducting such program;section 3027(a)(9) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: not less than $150,000 and not more than 4 percent of the amount allotted to the State for carrying out part B, shall be available for conducting outreach demonstration projects under ; andsection 3058e of this title

children.label: D
children.text: the remainder of such allotment shall be available to such State only for paying such percentage as the State agency determines, but not more than 85 percent of the cost of supportive services, senior centers, and nutrition services under this subchapter provided in the State as part of a comprehensive and coordinated system in planning and service areas for which there is an area plan approved by the State agency.

The non-Federal share shall be in cash or in kind. In determining the amount of the non-Federal share, the Assistant Secretary may attribute fair market value to services and facilities contributed from non-Federal sources.
